expectations.text: Classifies UserService, Postgres, AuditLogService all as Docker (internal)
expectations.evidence: Rows 1, 2, 5 of decision table classify UserService, Postgres, and AuditLogService as Internal with Docker strategy.

expectations.text: Classifies Stripe, SendGrid, OpenAI all as Mock (3rd-party)
expectations.evidence: Rows 3, 4, 6 classify Stripe, SendGrid, and OpenAI as 3rd-party vendor with Mock strategy.

expectations.text: States 'never mock internal services' / 'never invert' rule
expectations.evidence: Line 3: '3rd-party → mock; internal services we operate → Docker. Never invert.' and line 13: 'Same rule as UserService — never mock our own service.'

expectations.text: Flags the :latest tag on buckit-user-api and requires a pinned tag
expectations.evidence: Line 9 uses '<pinned-tag>' (not latest), and line 25 explicitly notes: 'never `latest` — note: replace the stated `buckit-user-api:latest` with a concrete SHA/version tag in the compose file'.

expectations.text: Prefers recorded fixtures over framework mocks for 3rd-party
expectations.evidence: Line 11: 'Recorded fixtures (e.g. WireMock.NET replay of real Stripe responses)'; line 14: 'Recorded fixtures via an IFraudScoreProvider interface with a RecordedOpenAIProvider replaying captured responses'.

expectations.text: Mentions that vendor sandboxes (real 3rd-party) belong in a separate nightly job, not main CI
expectations.evidence: Line 14 mentions 'a nightly vendor-smoke job can hit real OpenAI separately... without gating PRs'; line 30: 'A separate nightly vendor-smoke job may hit the real Stripe sandbox... but it never gates PR merges.'
